subject: Project Management
title: What 3.4 Defects Per Million Means
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: What 3.4 Defects Per Million Means.

hook.text: At three sigma quality, you get 66,807 defects per million. At six sigma, you get 3.4. The difference is staggering.

buildup.text: Three sigma means 99.73% good. Six sigma means 99.99966% good. That tiny gap is enormous at scale.

discovery.text: At three sigma, an airline would lose 2 bags per flight. At six sigma, it's 3.4 bags per million flights.

twist.text: Most companies operate between three and four sigma. Getting to six requires fundamentally rethinking processes.

climax.text: Six Sigma isn't about perfection — it's about making imperfection so rare it's negligible.

punchline.text: 99.9% sounds great. Six Sigma says it's not enough.

quiz.question: How does three sigma compare to six sigma?
options: Three sigma has 66,807 defects per million; six sigma has 3.4
Three sigma is better than six sigma
